risk_parameters.security_deposit_cap: 2 months rent for residential, 6 months for commercial (MTA & Section 24 MRC)
risk_parameters.rent_increase_cap: As per written agreement; 3 months written notice required for hike (MTA Guidelines)
risk_parameters.overstay_penalty: 2x rent for first two months, 4x rent subsequently (MTA Guidelines)

risk_parameters.security_deposit_cap: 2 months rent for residential, 6 months for commercial (MTA Guidelines)
risk_parameters.rent_increase_cap: 3 months written notice required for hike (MTA Guidelines)

state_laws: Telangana Buildings (Lease, Rent and Eviction) Control Act, 1960

risk_parameters.security_deposit_cap: Usually equivalent to 2-3 months rent, though legally negotiable.
risk_parameters.eviction_notice_min: 1 month written notice unless lease expires naturally.
risk_parameters.rent_increase_cap: Fair rent principles apply; unreasonable hikes can be contested via Rent Controller.
risk_parameters.overstay_penalty: Tenant holds over at their own risk; landlord can file for eviction and mesne profits.

central_laws: Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020

risk_parameters.wage_definition: Basic + DA must be at least 50% of total CTC (Code on Wages)
risk_parameters.gratuity_eligibility: 1 year for Fixed-Term Employees, 5 years for permanent (Code on Social Security)
risk_parameters.leave_entitlement: Eligible for annual leave after 180 days of work (OSHWC Code)
risk_parameters.max_working_hours: 9 hours/day, 48 hours/week (Maharashtra Shops Act & OSHWC Code)
